fields.name: J.K. Rowling
fields.description:
One of the most successful British authors in history, the creator of the monumental «Harry Potter» fantasy series, a 7-book masterpiece with a global circulation exceeding 600 million copies.

Born on July 31, 1965, in Yate, England. She conceived the idea for her famous series in 1990 while stuck on a delayed train journey from Manchester to London. Over the next few years, while living as a struggling single mother in Edinburgh, she wrote her debut novel. In 1997, she published «Harry Potter and the Philosopher’s Stone», which changed children's literature forever.

The series quickly turned into an unprecedented global phenomenon. Every new book smashed sales records, selling millions of copies within the first 24 hours of release. In 2001, she received the Order of the British Empire (OBE) for her services to children's literature. Today, besides her magical Wizarding World, she writes critically acclaimed contemporary crime fiction under the pen name Robert Galbraith.

fields.name: Agatha Christie
fields.description:
The undisputed "Queen of Crime" and the best-selling novelist of all time, the author of 66 detective novels with a staggering total circulation of over 2 billion copies worldwide.

Born on September 15, 1890, in Torquay, England. During the First World War, she worked at a hospital dispensary, acquiring a deep knowledge of medicines and poisons that later fueled her plots. In 1920, she published her debut novel, «The Mysterious Affair at Styles», introducing the world to the legendary Belgian detective Hercule Poirot.

True stardom arrived in 1926 with the publication of the groundbreaking thriller «The Murder of Roger Ackroyd». Christie’s books became an international phenomenon, loved for their brilliant red herrings and iconic characters like Miss Jane Marple. In 1971, she was named a Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire for her immense contribution to literature.

She passed away on January 12, 1976, leaving behind a timeless literary legacy that still keeps readers guessing.

fields.name: Andrzej Sapkowski
fields.description:
One of the world's most famous fantasy writers, the creator of the legendary «The Witcher» saga, which spans multiple books with a total circulation of over 30 million copies worldwide.

Born on June 21, 1948, in Łódź, Poland. Sapkowski graduated from the University of Łódź, where he studied economics, and worked for years as a senior foreign trade representative. In 1986, he wrote his first short story, «The Witcher» (Wiedźmin), for a contest held by the Polish science fiction and fantasy magazine Fantastyka, instantly capturing the readers' hearts.

Real success came in the early 1990s with the publication of his short story collections and the subsequent five-novel saga about Geralt of Rivia. Sapkowski’s grim yet witty dark fantasy universe became a global bestseller. In 1997, he fully committed to his writing career, winning numerous prestigious awards, including the World Fantasy Award for Life Achievement in 2016. Today, his books inspire record-breaking video games and high-budget television adaptations.

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone (Harry Potter, Book 1)
fields.description: Harry Potter has never even heard of Hogwarts when the letters start dropping on the doormat at number four, Privet Drive. Addressed in green ink on yellowish parchment with a purple seal, they are swiftly confiscated by his grisly aunt and uncle. Then, on Harry's eleventh birthday, a great beetle-eyed giant of a man called Rubeus Hagrid bursts in with some astonishing news: Harry Potter is a wizard, and he has a place at Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry. An incredible adventure is about to begin.

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets (Harry Potter, Book 2)
fields.description: Harry Potter can’t wait for his holidays with the dire Dursleys to end. But a small, self-punishing house-elf warns Harry of mortal danger awaiting him at Hogwarts. Returning to the castle nevertheless, Harry hears a rumour about a Chamber of Secrets, holding unknown horrors for wizards of Muggle parentage. Now someone is casting spells that turn people to stone, and a terrible warning is found painted on the wall. The chief suspect – always in the wrong place – is Harry. But something much more terrifying has yet to be unleashed. These new editions of the classic and internationally bestselling, multi-award-winning series feature instantly pick-up-able new jackets by Jonny Duddle, with huge child appeal, to bring Harry Potter to the next generation of readers. It’s time to PASS THE MAGIC ON …

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban (Harry Potter, Book 3)
fields.description: When the Knight Bus crashes through the darkness and screeches to a halt in front of him, it's the start of another far from ordinary year at Hogwarts for Harry Potter. Sirius Black, escaped mass-murderer and follower of Lord Voldemort, is on the run - and they say he is coming after Harry. In his first ever Divination class, Professor Trelawney sees an omen of death in Harry's tea leaves . But perhaps most terrifying of all are the Dementors patrolling the school grounds, with their soul-sucking Kiss .

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire (Harry Potter, Book 4)
fields.description: The Triwizard Tournament is to be held at Hogwarts. Only wizards who are over seventeen are allowed to enter - but that doesn't stop Harry dreaming that he will win the competition. Then at Hallowe'en, when the Goblet of Fire makes its selection, Harry is amazed to find his name is one of those that the magical cup picks out. He will face death-defying tasks, dragons and Dark wizards, but with the help of his best friends, Ron and Hermione, he might just make it through - alive!

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix (Harry Potter, Book 5)
fields.description: Dark times have come to Hogwarts. After the Dementors' attack on his cousin Dudley, Harry Potter knows that Voldemort will stop at nothing to find him. There are many who deny the Dark Lord's return, but Harry is not alone: a secret order gathers at Grimmauld Place to fight against the Dark forces. Harry must allow Professor Snape to teach him how to protect himself from Voldemort's savage assaults on his mind. But they are growing stronger by the day and Harry is running out of time .

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince (Harry Potter, Book 6)
fields.description: Suspicion and fear blow through the wizarding world as news of the Dark Lord's attack on the Ministry of Magic spreads. Harry has not told anyone about the future predicted by the prophecy in the Department of Mysteries, nor how deeply what happened to Sirius Black affected him. He's desperate for Professor Dumbledore to arrive and take him away from the Dursley's - but Hogwarts may not be the safe haven from Voldemort's Dark Forces that it once was.

fields.name: Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows (Harry Potter, Book 7)
fields.description: As he climbs into the sidecar of Hagrid's motorbike and takes to the skies, leaving Privet Drive for the last time, Harry Potter knows that Lord Voldemort and the Death Eaters are not far behind. The protective charm that has kept Harry safe until now is broken, but he cannot keep hiding. The Dark Lord is breathing fear into everything Harry loves, and to stop him Harry will have to find and destroy the remaining Horcruxes. The final battle must begin - Harry must stand and face his enemy.

fields.name: The Murder of Roger Ackroyd: A Hercule Poirot Mystery
fields.description:
Roger Ackroyd knew too much. He knew that the woman he loved had poisoned her brutal first husband. He suspected also that someone had been blackmailing her. Then, tragically, came the news that she had taken her own life with an apparent drug overdose.

However the evening post brought Roger one last fatal scrap of information, but before he could finish reading the letter, he was stabbed to death. Luckily one of Roger’s friends and the newest resident to retire to this normally quiet village takes over—none other than Monsieur Hercule Poirot.

fields.name: The Last Wish: Illustrated Edition (The Witcher, 1)
fields.description:
The perfect entry point into the Witcher universe. This collection of spellbinding short stories introduces Geralt of Rivia as he travels the Continent, hunting down strigas, vampires, and ghouls for coin. Yet, he quickly learns that the most monstrous beasts are often those hiding beneath a human face. This volume also marks the beginning of the tempestuous, fate-bound romance between the witcher and the beautiful, dangerous sorceress Yennefer of Vengerberg.

Background (Short Story Collections)

fields.name: Sword of Destiny: Illustrated Edition (The Witcher, 2)
fields.description:
Description: The second collection of tales that lays the groundwork for the epic events to come. As Geralt continues his lonely crusade against monsters, his own life undergoes a dramatic shift. He finds himself face-to-face with his Destiny in the form of Ciri, a young princess of a fallen kingdom. Packed with ancient prophecies, elven secrets, and bitter philosophical dilemmas, this book questions whether there is still a place for witchers in a rapidly changing world.

Background (Short Story Collections)

fields.name: Blood of Elves (The Witcher, 3)
fields.description:
The first full-length novel that kicks off the main epic saga. As the threat of a devastating war with the ruthless Nilfgaardian Empire looms over the Continent, a young girl named Ciri becomes the target of international espionage. Possessing a mysterious and terrifying magical heritage, she must be protected at all costs. Geralt takes her to the witcher stronghold of Kaer Morhen to train her in the art of the sword, while the sorceress Triss Merigold tries to keep her awakening powers under control.

The Main Saga (The Ciri and Geralt Cycle)

fields.name: The Time of Contempt (The Witcher, 4)
fields.description:
Political intrigue and dangerous conspiracies among the world's most powerful mages reach a boiling point. The Continent plunges into the chaos of a brand-new war when a bloody mutiny erupts during a grand gathering of wizards on the island of Thanedd. Severely wounded while trying to protect his loved ones, Geralt is separated from Yennefer, while Ciri finds herself entirely alone, fighting for survival in the deadly Korath desert.

The Main Saga (The Ciri and Geralt Cycle)

fields.name: Baptism of Fire (The Witcher, 5)
fields.description:
While the Continent burns in the fires of war, a recovering Geralt sets out on a desperate quest to find and rescue a missing Ciri. Along the way, a colorful and fiercely loyal band of companions rallies around him: the poet Dandelion, the fierce archer Milva, the Nilfgaardian knight Cahir, the dwarf Zoltan Chivay, and the enigmatic medic Regis. This is a story of true friendship, honor, and the baptism by fire they must all endure for the sake of an innocent child.

The Main Saga (The Ciri and Geralt Cycle)

fields.name: The Tower of Swallows (The Witcher, 6)
fields.description:
The world believes Ciri is either dead or captured by the Emperor, but she is actually recovering from severe wounds in a remote swamp under the care of an old hermit. Having survived betrayal, slavery, and the cruelty of the "Rats" bandit gang, Ciri gathers her strength to face her hunters and the merciless assassin Leo Bonart. Her only chance at salvation lies in finding the legendary Tower of the Swallow—a gateway to other worlds.

The Main Saga (The Ciri and Geralt Cycle)

fields.name: The Lady of the Lake (The Witcher, 7)
fields.description:
The grand, dramatic conclusion to the world-famous saga. Having mastered her ability to travel through space and time, Ciri desperately searches for the way back to her home world. Meanwhile, the armies of the Northern Kingdoms clash in a final, apocalyptic battle against the legions of Nilfgaard at Brenna. As Geralt and Yennefer storm the castle of the villainous mage Vilgefortz, the fates of all characters collide in a bittersweet and unforgettable finale.

The Main Saga (The Ciri and Geralt Cycle)

fields.name: Season of Storms (The Witcher, 8)
fields.description:
A standalone companion novel set during the events of the very first book. Geralt finds his signature witcher swords stolen, leaving him vulnerable and weaponless. In his quest to recover them, the White Wolf is dragged into courtly intrigues, faces the horrific experiments of rogue mages, and experiences a passionate affair with the sorceress Lytta Neyd, known as Coral. A fantastic standalone adventure in the style of Sapkowski’s earliest work.

Prequel

fields.name: Crossroads of Ravens (The Witcher, 0)
fields.description:
The latest standalone prequel to the legendary saga. Years of brutal training are over, and eighteen-year-old Geralt of Rivia leaves the safety of Kaer Morhen to hit the Witcher’s Trail for the very first time. Not yet the famous White Wolf, he is inexperienced, prone to mistakes, and just learning to survive in a world that fears and hates mutants. A gripping tale of first contracts, youthful idealism, and the dawn of the world's greatest monster hunter.

Prequel
